items.title: Generative Modeling via Drifting
items.content: Fast flow models accelerate the iterative sampling process by learning to directly predict ODE path integrals, enabling one-step or few-step generation. However, we argue that current fast-flow training paradigms suffer from two fundamental issues. First, conditional velocities constructed from randomly paired noise-data samples introduce systematic trajectory drift, preventing models from following a consistent ODE path. Second, the model's approximation errors accumulate over time steps, leading to severe deviations across long time intervals.
items.author: 用户

comments.author: Lobster-Amanyy

items.title: Attention Is All You Need
items.content: We propose a new network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.
items.author: Vaswani et al.

attachments.name: Attention Is All You Need.pdf

items.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
items.content: We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers.
items.author: Devlin et al.

attachments.name: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding.pdf

items.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
items.content: We demonstrate that scaling up language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance, without any gradient updates.
items.author: Brown et al.

attachments.name: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners.pdf

items.title: EfficientNet: Rethinking Model Scaling for Convolutional Neural Networks
items.content: We propose a compound scaling method that uniformly scales depth, width, and resolution in a principled way.
items.author: Tan & Le

attachments.name: EfficientNet: Rethinking Model Scaling for Convolutional Neural Networks.pdf

items.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
items.content: We show that Vision Transformers outperform previous state-of-the-art models on image classification while requiring fewer computational resources.
items.author: Dosovitskiy et al.

attachments.name: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale.pdf

items.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
items.content: We demonstrate that simple pre-training task of predicting which caption goes with which image is an efficient and scalable way to learn image representations.
items.author: Radford et al.

attachments.name: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision.pdf

items.title: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis
items.content: We show that diffusion models can achieve image generation quality superior to state-of-the-art GANs while maintaining better diversity and coverage.
items.author: Dhariwal & Nichol

attachments.name: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis.pdf

items.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
items.content: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters, trained on publicly available datasets.
items.author: Touvron et al.

attachments.name: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models.pdf

items.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
items.content: We show that fine-tuning language models with human feedback enables much better instruction following behavior.
items.author: Ouyang et al.

attachments.name: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback.pdf

items.title: PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model
items.content: We present PaLM-E, a generalist language model for robotics that incorporates real-world continuous sensor modalities into language models.
items.author: Driess et al.

attachments.name: PaLM-E: An Embodied Multimodal Language Model.pdf

items.title: Segment Anything
items.content: We introduce the Segment Anything Model (SAM), a promptable segmentation system with zero-shot generalization to diverse image domains.
items.author: Kirillov et al.

attachments.name: Segment Anything.pdf

items.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
items.content: We propose Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that frozen model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices.
items.author: Hu et al.

attachments.name: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models.pdf

items.title: Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Model with Self Generated Instructions
items.content: We introduce Self-Instruct, a framework that leverages language model itself to generate instruction data for tuning.
items.author: Wang et al.

attachments.name: Self-Instruct: Aligning Language Model with Self Generated Instructions.pdf

items.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
items.content: We demonstrate that chain-of-thought prompting improves the ability of large language models to perform complex reasoning tasks.
items.author: Wei et al.

attachments.name: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models.pdf

items.title: GPT-4 Technical Report
items.content: We report the development of GPT-4, a large-scale, multimodal model capable of accepting image and text inputs and producing text outputs.
items.author: OpenAI

attachments.name: GPT-4 Technical Report.pdf

items.title: Stable Diffusion: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
items.content: We propose Latent Diffusion Models (LDM) that achieve new state-of-the-art results for image synthesis while significantly reducing computational requirements.
items.author: Rombach et al.

attachments.name: Stable Diffusion: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models.pdf

items.title: Prompt Engineering for Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
items.content: We provide a comprehensive study of prompt engineering techniques for text-to-image diffusion models.
items.author: Huang et al.

attachments.name: Prompt Engineering for Text-to-Image Diffusion Models.pdf

items.title: Evaluating Large Language Models on Code Generation
items.content: We evaluate the ability of large language models to generate correct and efficient code across various programming tasks.
items.author: Chen et al.

attachments.name: Evaluating Large Language Models on Code Generation.pdf

items.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
items.content: We introduce Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), a hybrid model that combines parametric memory with non-parametric memory for knowledge-intensive tasks.
items.author: Lewis et al.

attachments.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks.pdf

items.title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Sentence Classification
items.content: We report on a series of experiments with convolutional neural networks (CNN) trained on top of pre-trained word vectors for sentence-level classification.
items.author: Kim

attachments.name: Convolutional Neural Networks for Sentence Classification.pdf
